name: Memoization Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
Why a Greedy Solution doesnГўВҖВҷt work?
The first approach that comes to our mind is greedy. A greedy solution will fail in this problem because there is no ГўВҖВҳuniformityГўВҖВҷ in data. While selecting a local better choice we may choose an item that will in the long term give less value.
As the greedy approach doesnГўВҖВҷt work, we will try to generate all possible combinations using recursion and select the combination which gives us the maximum value in the given constraints.
Steps to form the recursive solution:
We will first form the recursive solution by the three points mentioned in 
Dynamic Programming Introduction.
Express the problem in terms of indexes:

We are given ГўВҖВҳnГўВҖВҷ items. Their weight is represented by the ГўВҖВҳwtГўВҖВҷ array and value by the ГўВҖВҳvalГўВҖВҷ array. So clearly one parameter will be ГўВҖВҳindГўВҖВҷ, i.e., the index up to which the array items are being considered.
There is one more parameter ГўВҖВңWГўВҖВқ. We need the capacity of the knapsack to decide whether we can pick an array item or not in the knapsack.
So, we can say that initially, we need to find f(n-1, W) where W is the overall capacity given to us. f(n-1, W) means we are finding the maximum value of items that the thief can steal from items with index 0 to n-1 with capacity W of the knapsack.


Base Cases:

If ind == 0, it means we are at the first item. Now, in an unbounded knapsack we can pick an item any number of times we want. As there is only one item left, we will pick for W/wt[0] times because we ultimately want to maximize the value of items while respecting the constraint of weight of the knapsack. The value added will be the product of the number of items picked and value of the individual item. Therefore we return (W/wt[0]) * val[0].


Try out all possible choices at a given index:

We need to generate all the subsequences. We will use the pick/non-pick technique as discussed in this video 
    Recursion on Subsequences.
  
We have two choices:
    
Exclude the current element in the subsequence: We first try to find a subsequence without considering the current index item. If we exclude the current item, the capacity of the bag will not be affected and the value added will be 0 for the current item. So we will call the recursive function f(ind-1, W).
Include the current element in the subsequence: We will try to find a subsequence by considering the current item to the knapsack. As we have included the item, the capacity of the knapsack will be updated to W-wt[ind] and the current itemГўВҖВҷs value (val[ind]) will also be added to the further recursive call answer.
Since there is an unlimited supply of items, we will not recursively call for f(ind-1, W-wt[ind]). Instead, we stay at the same index and call f(ind, W-wt[ind]).
Note: We consider the current item only if its weight is less than or equal to the capacity W of the knapsack.




Return the maximum of take and notTake:

Since we need to maximize the value, the final result will be max(take, notTake).


Steps to memoize a recursive solution:

If we draw the recursion tree, we will see that there are overlapping subproblems. To convert a recursive solution to memoization:
    
Create a dp array of size [n][W+1]. The size of the input array is ГўВҖВҳnГўВҖВҷ, so the index will always lie between ГўВҖВҳ0ГўВҖВҷ and ГўВҖВҳn-1ГўВҖВҷ. The capacity can take any value between ГўВҖВҳ0ГўВҖВҷ and ГўВҖВҳWГўВҖВҷ. Therefore we take the dp array as dp[n][W+1].
Initialize the dp array to -1.
Whenever we want to find the answer of particular parameters (say f(ind, target)), we first check whether the answer is already calculated (dp[ind][target] != -1). If yes, simply return the value from the dp array.
If not, then we are finding the answer for the given parameters for the first time. We compute it using the recursive relation, and before returning, we store it in dp[ind][target].

complexities.time: O(n ГғВ— n) Each subproblem (i, length) is computed once.
complexities.space: O(n ГғВ— n),We use a 2D DP table for memoization.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Function to compute max value by cutting rod using memoization
    def rodCutting(self, price, n):
        # Initialize a 2D dp table with -1
        dp = [[-1] * (n + 1) for _ in range(n)]

        # Call helper from last index
        return self.helper(n - 1, n, price, dp)

    # Recursive helper function with memoization
    def helper(self, i, length, price, dp):
        # Base case: only 1 piece of length 1
        if i == 0:
            return length * price[0]

        # Return precomputed value
        if dp[i][length] != -1:
            return dp[i][length]

        # Option 1: not take
        notTake = self.helper(i - 1, length, price, dp)

        # Option 2: take if rod fits
        take = float('-inf')
        rodLength = i + 1
        if rodLength <= length:
            take = price[i] + self.helper(i, length - rodLength, price, dp)

        # Store and return result
        dp[i][length] = max(take, notTake)
        return dp[i][length]

# Driver code
price = [1, 5, 8, 9, 10, 17, 17, 20]
n = 8

sol = Solution()
print("The maximum obtainable value is:", sol.rodCutting(price, n))

name: Tabulation Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
To convert the memoization approach to a tabulation one, create a dp array with the same size as done in memoization. We can initialize it as 0.
First, we need to initialize the base conditions of the recursive solution.
At ind==0, we are considering the first element, so we will assign its value as (i/wt[0]) * val[0], where i will iterate from 0 to W.Next, we are done for the first row, so our ГўВҖВҳindГўВҖВҷ variable will move from 1 to n-1, whereas our ГўВҖВҳcapГўВҖВҷ variable will move from 0 to ГўВҖВҳWГўВҖВҷ. We will set the nested loops to traverse the dp array.Inside the nested loops we will apply the recursive logic to find the answer of the cell.When the nested loop execution has ended, we will return dp[n-1][W] as our answer.

complexities.time: O(N * W),Because we have a nested loop iterating through all `n` items and all `W` capacities.
complexities.space: O(N * W),We are using a 2D DP table of size `n` by `W+1` to store intermediate results, and we eliminated recursion stack space.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Function to solve the rod cutting problem
    def rodCutting(self, price, n):
        # Initialize a 2D DP table with dimensions [n][n+1]
        # dp[ind][length] represents the maximum price obtainable
        # by considering rod pieces up to index 'ind' and total rod length 'length'
        dp = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(n)]
        
        # Base case: filling the first row (index 0)
        # If only the first piece is available, we can cut 'length' times
        # and total value will be price[0] * length
        for length in range(n + 1):
            dp[0][length] = price[0] * length

        # Fill the DP table for other indices
        for ind in range(1, n):
            for length in range(1, n + 1):
                
                # Case when the current piece is not taken
                notTaken = dp[ind - 1][length]
                
                # Case when the current piece is taken
                taken = float('-inf')
                
                # The actual rod length corresponding to this index
                rodLength = ind + 1
                
                # If this rod length can fit, consider taking it
                if rodLength <= length:
                    taken = price[ind] + dp[ind][length - rodLength]
                
                # Store the maximum of taken and notTaken
                dp[ind][length] = max(notTaken, taken)
        
        # The last cell contains the maximum obtainable value
        return dp[n - 1][n]


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Prices for each rod piece length
    price = [2, 4, 6, 8]
    # Length of the rod (equal to number of price entries)
    n = len(price)
    
    # Create an instance of Solution
    sol = Solution()
    
    # Get the maximum obtainable value
    result = sol.rodCutting(price, n)
    
    # Print result
    print("The Maximum value is", result)

name: Space Optimization Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
If we closely look the relation,
dp[ind][cap] =В  max(dp[ind-1][cap] ,dp[ind][cap-wt[ind]]
We see that to calculate a value of a cell of the dp array, we need only the previous row values (say prev). So, we donГўВҖВҷt need to store an entire array. Hence we can space optimize it.
We will be space optimizing this solution using only one row.
If we clearly see the values required:В  dp[ind-1][cap] and dp[ind-1][cap - wt[ind]], we can say that if we are at a column cap, we will only require the values shown in the blue box(of the same column) from the previous row and other values will be from the cur row itself. So why do we need to store an entire array for it?
If we need only one value from the prev row, there is no need to store an entire row. We can work a bit smarter.
We can use the cur row itself to store the required value in the following way:
We somehow make sure that the previous value( say preValue) is available to us in some manner ( we will discuss later how we got the value).Now, let us say that we want to find the value of cell cur[3], by going through the relation we find that we need a preValue and one value from the cur row.We see that to calculate the cur[3] element, we need only a single variable (preValue). The catch is that we can initially place this preValue at the position cur[3] (before finding its updated value) and later while calculating for the current rowГўВҖВҷs cell cur[3], the value present there automatically serves as the preValue and we can use it to find the required cur[3] value. ( If there is any confusion please see the code).After calculating the cur[3] value we store it at the cur[3] position so this cur[3] will automatically serve as preValue for the next row. In this way, we space-optimize the tabulation approach by just using one row.

complexities.time: O(N * W), We iterate through N items, and for each item we process W weight capacities.
complexities.space: O(W),We only use a 1D array of size W to store intermediate results, eliminating the need for 2D DP or recursion stack.

code.python:
class Solution:
    # Function to solve the rod cutting problem
    def rodCutting(self, price, n):
        # Create two 1D arrays for space optimization
        prev = [0] * (n + 1)
        cur = [0] * (n + 1)
        
        # Base case: if only the first piece is available
        for length in range(n + 1):
            prev[length] = price[0] * length
        
        # Fill DP rows for each piece length
        for ind in range(1, n):
            for length in range(1, n + 1):
                # Case 1: Do not take current piece
                notTaken = prev[length]
                
                # Case 2: Take current piece if possible
                taken = float('-inf')
                rodLength = ind + 1
                if rodLength <= length:
                    taken = price[ind] + cur[length - rodLength]
                
                # Store maximum value
                cur[length] = max(notTaken, taken)
            
            # Update prev row with current row
            prev = cur[:]
        
        # Final answer is stored in prev[n]
        return prev[n]


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    price = [2, 4, 6, 8]
    n = len(price)
    
    sol = Solution()
    print("The Maximum value is", sol.rodCutting(price, n))
